mappings.rationaleAI: Core ecology covering population dynamics (A.3, A.4), interspecies interactions (B.2), community structure (C.1), energy flow (C.2), and succession (C.3).
Natural selection and speciation address population genetics (A.2) and evolutionary aspects of interactions (B.3).
Symbiotic associations address positive interspecific interactions (B.2.c) and community symbiosis (C.1.c).
Logistic growth and Lotka-Volterra dynamics address population growth (A.3), fluctuations (A.4), and interspecific interactions (B.2).
Invasive species address population dispersal (A.5), negative interactions (B.2.b), and succession disruption (C.3).
Reef symbiosis exemplifies mutualism (B.2.c), community structure (C.1), symbiotic interactions (C.1.c), and species diversity (C.1.d).
Forest dynamics cover stratification (C.1.a), energy flow (C.2), succession (C.3), landscape patterns (C.4), and biome classification (C.5).
Marine ecosystems address populations (A), interspecific interactions (B.2), and community structure (C).
Biodiversity threats address populations under stress (A), interaction cascades (B), and species diversity (C.1.d).
Microbial communities exemplify positive interspecific interactions (B.2.c).
Mass extinctions address population fluctuations (A.4) and succession disruption (C.3).
Plant diversity addresses community structure (C.1) and community classification (C.5).
Genetic variation addresses population genetic differences (A.2.a).
